---
title: "webR pilot — runnable cells in the browser"
format: live-html
engine: knitr
webr:
  packages:
    - itssl
    - ggplot2
  repos:
    - https://danmaclean.r-universe.dev
    - https://repo.r-wasm.org
---

{{< include ./_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}

This is a throwaway feasibility pilot for Phase 2 (not wired into the book).
The code cells below run **in the reader's browser** via webR — no install, no
server. `itssl` (with its bundled potato datasets) is pulled from
`danmaclean.r-universe.dev`; its dependencies come from `repo.r-wasm.org`.
Edit any cell and press *Run*.

## Real data, in the browser

A genuine plant-pathology experiment — potato scab vs sulfur dose — loaded
straight from the package:

```{webr}
data(potato_scab, package = "itssl")
head(potato_scab)
```

## Every test is a slope

```{webr}
fit <- lm(infection ~ sulfur, data = potato_scab)
summary(fit)
```

## A plot, drawn client-side

```{webr}
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(potato_scab, aes(sulfur, infection)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "sulfur dose", y = "% scab") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## An `itssl` helper still works under webR

```{webr}
its_axplusb_time(a = 2, b = 1)
```
